THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT DEFENDANT FRIEND, VINCENT AN INDIVIDUAL RECOVER FROM PLAINTIFF J GARCIA CARRION, SA, A CORPORATION ORGANIZED AND EXISTING UNDER THE LAWS OF THE KINGDOM OF SPAIN CIV USA INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION $2,743,903.00 PRINCIPAL, TOTAL JUDGMENT $2,743,903.00, PLUS 10% INTEREST THEREON FROM AND AFTER JULY 31, 2018 (JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY - SEE SCANNED DOC

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS VINCENT FRIEND AND FRIEND WINE MARKETING, INC.'S PETITION TO CONFIRM CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. MR. FRIEND IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT IN HIS FAVOR AGAINST RESPONDENTS J GARCIA CARRION, S.A. AND CIV USA INC., JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY, IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,743,903, PLUS 10% INTEREST THEREON FROM AND AFTER JULY 31, 2018 COMPOUNDED ANNUALLY. ORDER AND JUDGMENT ARE SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER AND JUDGMENT FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT CONFIRMS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS TO CORDELINA WINE CO AND FRIEND WINE MARKETING INC AND WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE IF THE PLAINTIFFS CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH A VIABLE CLAIM BASED ON POST-PARTIAL FINAL AWARD CONDUCT THAT ENTITLES THE PLAINTIFFS OR SOME OF THEM, TO THE STOCK IN THE FRIEND COMPANY. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert A. Rivas, Esq., ropers, Majeski, Kohn & Bentley, telephone 650-364-8200, appearing for Plaintiff;
Mark Schickman, Esq., Freeland, Cooper, Foreman, Telephone 415-541-0200, appearing for Defendant vincent Friend, et al;

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Vincent Friend, Friend Wine Marketing, Inc., and Cordelina Wine Company, LLC's motion to strike portions of the second amended complaint filed by plaintiffs J Garcia Carrion, SA and CIV USA, Inc. is off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on defendants' demurrer to the second amended complaint and plaintiffs' dismissal of the third and sixth causes of action.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert A. Rivas, Esq., Ropers, Majeski, Kohn & Bentley, telephone 650-364-8200, appearing for Plaintiff (CourtCall);
Mark Schickman, Esq., Freeland, Cooper, Foreman, Telephone 415-541-0200, appearing for Defendant Vincent Friend, et al;

RULING - ARGUED: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Vincent Friend, Friend Wine Marketing, Inc., and Cordelina Wine Company, LLC's demurrer to all six causes of action in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiffs J Garcia Carrion, SA and CIV USA, Inc. is sustained without leave to amend as to the first, second, fourth, and fifth causes of action for breach of contract, promissory estoppel, injunctive relief, and specific performance and off calendar as moot as to the third and sixth causes of action for unfair business practices and conversion. Plaintiffs have not alleged, nor does it appear that they are able to allege, ultimate facts supporting the existence of either an agreement or a clear and unambiguous promise to transfer the FWM stock to JGC and thus their claims seeking the transfer of the stock fail as a matter of law. As a matter of law, Exhibit A to the second amended complaint does not show either an enforceable agreement or a clear and unambiguous promise to do or not do anything and any oral representations of the same ilk fail for the same reason. Plaintiffs have dismissed the third and sixth causes of action. To the extent that plaintiffs' other causes of action rest in whole or in part on allegations regarding the failure to transfer a domain name and failure to withdraw brand registrations, per plaintiffs' acknowledgment that they are no longer pursuing those allegations, those causes of action or portions of causes of action are moot.
Order and amendment to the to July 10, 2018 judgment is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)

Defendant American Arbitration Association'S Demurrer To The First Amended Complaint. (Per Ex Parte Order Filed 8/13/18)
Off Calendar Aug-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Robert A. Rivas of RMKB, tel. No. 1-213-312-2018 appearing for the plaintiff;
Atty. Mark I. Schickman of Freeland Cooper & Foreman LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-541-0200
appearing for the Defendants;
Atty. Bruce B. Kelson of Schnader, Tel. No. 1-415-364-6744 appearing for Defendant AAA.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant American Arbitration Association's demurrer to the fourth and fifth causes of action in the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to both causes of action. AAA's request for judicial notice of the Escrow Agreement, the Partial Final Award, and Procedural Order No. 11 is granted as to all three documents. Based on those documents, the fourth and fifth causes of action fail as a matter of law since those documents establish that AAA did not breach any duty to plaintiffs J Garcia Carrion, SA and CIV USA, Inc. when AAA disbursed the funds it held per the Escrow Agreement to defendants per the directions of the arbitrators in the Partial Final Award. As a matter of law, once the Partial Final Award was issued, AAA had no obligation to retain the funds it held per the Escrow Agreement pending an agreement by the parties to the arbitration as to the disposition of those funds. Plaintiffs' request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits A, B, and F and denied as to the remaining exhibits. As to the exhibits which the court has not taken judicial notice, the court has considered the contents of those exhibits as a proffer of what plaintiffs would allege in an amended complaint if they were granted leave to amend. Exhibits C-E and G-J do not show that plaintiffs are able to allege any viable claims against AAA because all of those exhibits pre-date, and as a matter of law any offers or admissions therein are superseded by, the Partial Final Award. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Atty. Robert A. Rivas of RMKB, tel. No. 1-213-312-2018 appearing for the plaintiff;
Atty. Mark I. Schickman of Freeland Cooper & Foreman LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-541-0200
appearing for the Defendants;
Atty. Bruce B. Kelson of Schnader, Tel. No. 1-415-364-6744 appearing for Defendant AAA.

RULING - A R G U E D; - The court confirms its tentative ruling as to Cordelina Wine Co and Friend Wine Marketing Inc and with 30 days leave to amend to allege if the plaintiffs can do so in good faith a viable claim based on post-partial final award conduct that entitles the plaintiffs or some of them, to the stock in the Friend company. Defendants Vincent Friend, Cordelina Wine Company and Friend Wine Marketing, Inc.'s demurrer to the first through third and sixth through eleventh causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiffs J Garcia Carrion, SA and CIV USA, Inc. is sustained with leave to amend as to all causes of action. The doctrine of claim preclusion bars all of plaintiffs' claims premised on their alleged ownership of FWM stock because the ownership of the stock was an issue "within the scope of the [arbitral] action, related to the subject-matter and relevant to the issues, so that it could have been raised, the judgment is conclusive on it despite the fact that it was not in fact expressly pleaded or otherwise urged." (Bucur v. Ahmad (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 175, 185). Because the third and sixth through eleventh causes of action are all premised on plaintiffs' ownership of the FWM, all of those claims fail. The first and second causes of action for breach of the escrow agreement and the implied good faith covenant therein are also barred by the doctrine of claim preclusion. Those claims are based on the allegation that the manner and timing of the distribution of the money held in escrow to defendants violated plaintiffs' contract rights. However, whether, when and how the money held in escrow should be distributed to defendants was or could have been litigated in the arbitration proceedings and in the proceedings before this court on the motion to confirm the arbitration award. The doctrine of claim preclusion now bars plaintiffs' claims raising those issues outside the arbitration or a post-arbitration motion to confirm, correct or vacate the arbitration award. Plaintiffs' request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits A, B, and F and denied as to the remaining exhibits. As to the exhibits which the court has not taken judicial notice, the court has considered the contents of those exhibits as a proffer of what plaintiffs would allege in an amended complaint if they were granted leave to amend. Exhibits C-E and G-J do not show that plaintiffs are able to allege any viable claims against demurring defendants because all of those exhibits pre-date, and as a matter of law any offers or admissions therein are superseded by, the Partial Final Award. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jul-10-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Rivas, Esq. 213-312-2018 of RMKB for Plaintiff J. Garcia Carrion.
Mark Schikman, Esq. 415-541-0200 of Freeland Cooper & Foreman LLP for Petitioner/Defendant VINCENT FRIEND, FRIEND WINE MARKETING, INC.
Bruce Kelson, Esq. 415-364-6744 of Schnader for Defendant American Arbitration Association.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER VINCENT FRIEND, FRIEND WINE MARKETING, INC.'s Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Petitioners Vincent Friend and Friend Wine Marketing, Inc.'s petition to confirm contractual arbitration award is granted. Mr. Friend is entitled to judgment in his favor against respondents J Garcia Carrion, S.A. and CIV USA Inc., jointly and severally, in the amount of $2,743,903, plus 10% interest thereon from and after July 31, 2018 compounded annually. Judicial review of contractual arbitration awards is limited to "those cases in which there exists a statutory ground to vacate or correct the award." (Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 28). JCG has failed to show that any of the statutory grounds apply. JCG's argument that the remedy the arbitrators awarded was specific performance of the 1992 Agreement buyout provision is not supported by the language of the award which expressly says that the sum awarded to Mr. Friend is for JCG's breach of the contract. (Partial Final Award pp. 9, 18, 31). Even if the measure of damages was legally erroneous as not being based on the difference between the buyout price and the market value of the stock and even if this resulted in a windfall to Mr. Friend, an error of law appearing on the face of the award and causing substantial injustice is not a ground to vacate an arbitration award. (Id.) Nor has JCG shown that any of the grounds for correcting a contractual arbitration award applies to this case. There was no evident miscalculation of figures by the arbitrators. The arbitrators did not exceed their powers because they had the authority to resolve the parties' disputes per the contract which gave them jurisdiction over "any controversy or claim arising out of or related to" the Agreement or its breach. (General Agreement Art. 10). Per Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. v. Intel Corp. (1994) 9 Cal.4th 362, 386, the award was "rationally related to the arbitrator's plausible findings as to the subject matter of the contract and the effects of the breach." (Partial Final Award pp. 2, 12). Nor is the correction sought by JCG a matter of form that does not affect the substance of the controversy. Whether Mr. Friend or JCG is entitled to the amount awarded to FWM substantially affects the merits of the controversy. Mr. Friend's conduct after the award was issued is not a ground for correction of the award.

Order and Judgment are signed. (see scanned order and judgment for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
